libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that call com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(Reference, Name, Context, Hashtable) or the PropertiesConfig overload, set System property `com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist` to the fully qualified ObjectFactory class name(s) used by the test before invoking the method, and clear/restore it afterward. Alternatively, call the overload that accepts an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set. Keep the whitelist narrow; do not use broad bypasses unless the test is specifically covering that behavior.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact declares optional integrations for Typesafe Config/HOCON, log4j 1.x, log4j2, and slf4j; those are not required for baseline utility coverage, but tests that directly exercise those integration classes may need matching optional test dependencies.
The whitelist is a JVM-global system property, so tests must restore it to avoid order-dependent behavior.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 can be meaningfully tested with the normal library dependency, but tests that exercise ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject overloads without an explicit allowed-factory Set now require the library's ObjectFactory whitelist system property.
